description: Native Swift iPhone MVP for a holistic women's health app centered on inside-out acne care, protein-forward macro tracking, reusable JSON food records, Gemini-assisted food and wellness workflows, supplements, lifestyle support, exercise logging, and manual health assessments. The app is local-first with optional iCloud sync, user-provided Gemini API key support, and a Ristoro-inspired Italian Art Deco visual system.

userStories.title: Create Swift App Baseline and Four-Tab Shell
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a native Swift iPhone app scaffold with the approved information architecture so that all MVP modules have a stable place to be implemented.
acceptanceCriteria: A Swift iPhone app project exists with a launchable app target.
The primary interface uses four bottom tabs labeled Lifestyle, Supplements, Macro, and Exercise.
A Settings entry point is available from a gear or profile control outside the bottom tab bar.
The app includes placeholder screens for each primary tab and Settings.
Typecheck passes
Build passes
Verify in iOS simulator that the app launches and the four tabs are navigable.
No unrelated files are modified
progress.txt is updated with result and notes
userStories.notes: Scaffolded with XcodeGen (project.yml -> HolisticHealth.xcodeproj). Four-tab TabView (Lifestyle/Supplements/Macro/Exercise) + Settings sheet from nav-bar gear. iOS 17 target, Swift 5 mode. Build + 2 unit tests pass; verified launch on iPhone 16 sim.

userStories.title: Implement Ristoro-Inspired SwiftUI Design System
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to follow the approved Italian Art Deco visual direction so that the experience feels calm, premium, and cohesive.
acceptanceCriteria: SwiftUI design tokens define aged cream paper, midnight navy ink, antique brass/gold, botanical verde, and restrained rosso colors.
Reusable SwiftUI components exist for framed cards, tags/chips, primary and secondary buttons, input fields, photo placeholders, ornamental rules, and tab treatments.
Typography choices follow the Ristoro direction using available iOS fonts or bundled fonts if added intentionally.
Core placeholder screens use the design system instead of default unstyled controls.
Accessibility contrast is checked for key text and controls.
Typecheck passes
Build passes
Verify in iOS simulator that components render without overlapping text on a standard iPhone viewport.
No unrelated files are modified
progress.txt is updated with result and notes
userStories.notes: Design tokens (Theme.swift) for paper/ink/gold/verde/rosso ported from Ristoro guide. Components: FramedCard, InkPanel, Primary/SecondaryButtonStyle, Chip/StatusTag, DecoTextField, OrnamentalRule, PhotoPlaceholder, SectionHeader, EyebrowText. Type mapped to iOS-native Futura (display) + Avenir Next (body). Nav/tab bar themed via ThemeAppearance. Build + 4 tests pass; sim verified no overlap.

userStories.title: Define Core Domain Models
userStories.description: As a developer, I want typed domain models for the MVP health data so that screens, persistence, and AI workflows share consistent structures.
acceptanceCriteria: Codable models exist for user profile, goals, consent records, macro library records, meal log entries, supplements, lifestyle practices, exercise sessions, symptom logs, acne logs, acne assessments, consult sessions, health assessments, AI settings, and API usage events.
Macro library records include canonical name, aliases, serving description, calories, protein grams, carbohydrate grams, fat grams, source, confidence, confirmation state, timestamps, and notes.
Daily meal log records are separate from reusable macro library records and can reference a library record when available.
Exercise records include category, activity where applicable, intensity, duration, timestamp, notes, and source.
Acne assessment records do not require storing the source image by default.
Typecheck passes
Build passes
No unrelated files are modified
progress.txt is updated with result and notes
userStories.notes: Codable models in HolisticHealth/Models: Macros (MacroNutrients, FoodSource, ConfirmationState, MealCategory, MacroLibraryRecord, MealLogEntry), UserProfile (+GoalArea, CyclePhase, ConsentType, ConsentRecord), Supplements (+SupplementLogEntry, NutrientSuggestion), Lifestyle (PracticeType, LifestylePractice, PracticeStatus, log), Exercise (category/intensity/SprintActivity/ExerciseSession), HealthLogs (HormoneSkinCheckIn, SymptomLog, AcneLog), Assessments (AcneAssessment-no image, ConsultSession, HealthAssessment, HealthReference, QAPair, AssessmentCategory), AISettings (GeminiModel+pricing, AISettings without key, AIFeature, APIUsageEvent). 9 round-trip/scaling tests pass.

userStories.title: Add Local JSON Storage and Import Export Foundation
userStories.description: As a user, I want my macro library and logs stored locally with JSON import and export so that the app can build a custom food database over time.
acceptanceCriteria: A local persistence service can read and write the editable JSON-backed macro library.
The storage layer supports saving, updating, deleting, and searching macro library records.
The app can import a saved JSON macro database and merge or replace records through an explicit user flow or service method.
The app can export the macro library JSON to a file or share target.
The storage design leaves a clear extension point for optional iCloud sync.
Typecheck passes
Build passes
Tests pass for JSON encode, decode, import, export, and record update behavior.
No unrelated files are modified
progress.txt is updated with result and notes
userStories.notes: Persistence in Services/Persistence: JSONCoding (iso8601 shared coders), DataPersisting protocol + FileDataStore (injectable baseDirectory for tests; StorageLocation enum documents iCloud extension point), MacroLibraryStore @MainActor ObservableObject with upsert/update/delete/clear/search/bestMatch + export(Data/File) + importLibrary(merge|replace, dedupe by id then normalized name). 8 storage tests (16 total) pass.

userStories.title: Build Onboarding and Health Profile
userStories.description: As a new user, I want to define hormone, skin, nutrition, supplement, stress, sleep, and wellness priorities so that the app can tailor the experience.
acceptanceCriteria: Onboarding captures goals across inside-out acne care, hormone health, cycle context, nutrition, supplements, stress, sleep, exercise, and mindfulness.
Users can skip optional questions and edit profile answers later.
Onboarding includes wellness-only safety language and does not claim to diagnose or treat medical conditions.
Saved profile data is available to Lifestyle, Supplements, Macro, Acne Review, Consult, and Health Assessment workflows.
Typecheck passes
Build passes
Verify in iOS simulator that onboarding can be completed and profile data persists after relaunch.
No unrelated files are modified
progress.txt is updated with result and notes
userStories.notes: OnboardingView: 4-step flow (welcome+safety, goals multi-select across all GoalAreas, optional cycle context w/ Skip, optional notes) -> ProfileStore.update marks onboardingCompleted. AppRootView gates onboarding vs RootView. ProfileEditView (Settings > Edit profile) edits later. ProfileStore @MainActor env object available app-wide; consents + reset included. SafetyText/SafetyNote shared wellness copy. FlowChips/WrapLayout added. 3 ProfileStore tests pass; sim verified onboarding renders + seeded profile skips to shell.

userStories.title: Implement Settings for Gemini Key, Model Selection, and Cost Tracking
userStories.description: As a user, I want to enter my Gemini API key, choose a model, and see usage costs so that AI features use my own configured access.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings includes Gemini API key entry with paste, show or hide, save, clear, and configured-state display.
The API key is stored securely in Keychain and is not hardcoded, logged, or included in exports.
Settings exposes user-selectable Gemini model options for AI workflows.
AI usage events store feature, model, timestamp, success or failure state, token usage when available, and estimated cost when pricing metadata is available.
Settings summarizes estimated daily, weekly, average, and total Gemini usage cost when data is available.
Typecheck passes
Build passes
Verify in iOS simulator that settings values persist and can be cleared.
No unrelated files are modified
progress.txt is updated with result and notes
userStories.notes: KeychainService (Security framework) stores Gemini key only in Keychain (kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlockThisDeviceOnly). AIConfigStore @MainActor env object: model selection persisted to ai_settings.json, hasAPIKey derived from Keychain, usage events to usage_events.json, costSummary (today/last7/avg-per-active-day/total) + static estimateCost from GeminiModel.pricing. GeminiSettingsView: key paste/show-hide/save/clear + configured state, model radio cards, cost panel. Key never in JSON/exports. 6 tests pass; sim verified key entry + model selection render. Added DEBUG-only HH_SCREEN router for screenshotting nav-gated screens.

userStories.title: Create Gemini AI Service Layer with Structured Responses
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a typed Gemini service layer so that food, acne, consult, supplement, lifestyle, and assessment features can share safe AI request behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: A typed AI service reads the selected model and API key from settings.

The service supports structured JSON response requests for food parsing, photo analysis, visualized-menu macros, acne assessment, consult questions, supplement suggestions, lifestyle suggestions, and health assessment reports.
The service records usage events and errors without exposing the API key.
The service has fallback behavior for missing key, unavailable model, rate limit, failed request, malformed response, and policy-blocked output.
Prompts constrain output to wellness education and forbid diagnosis, treatment plans, medication changes, and urgent-care triage.
Typecheck passes
Build passes
Tests pass for request construction, missing-key handling, malformed-response handling, and usage logging.
No unrelated files are modified
progress.txt is updated with result and notes
userStories.notes: GeminiService @MainActor generic generate<T:Decodable>(feature,userText,images,schema) -> reads model+key from AIConfigStore, applies AIGuardrails system instruction, requests structured JSON (responseMimeType+responseSchema), records success/failure usage events (key never in body/logs - only in URL query), maps errors to AIError (missingKey/modelUnavailable/rateLimited/requestFailed/malformed/policyBlocked/transport) with safe fallback messages. HTTPClient protocol + URLSession impl + mock. JSONSchema builder (OPENAPI uppercase). AIGuardrails forbids diagnosis/treatment/meds/triage. 7 tests pass (construction, missing-key, malformed, usage logging, 429, safety-block, 404).

userStories.title: Build Macro Tab Food Data Module
userStories.description: As a user, I want a Macro tab with Add Food, New Food, Log Meal, Food Log, Daily Totals, and Visualize Food sections so that food tracking is centered on protein, carbs, and fat.
acceptanceCriteria: Macro tab includes Add Food, New Food, Log Meal, Food Log, Daily Totals, and Visualize Food entry points or sections.
The UI emphasizes protein, carbohydrates, and fat; calories may appear only as secondary food data.
The module excludes calorie deficit, burn rate, weight-loss countdown, Better Choices, dynamic burn rate, burn and weight, and how-are-you-feeling sections.
Food Log rows show food name, timestamp, serving amount and size, secondary calories, protein, carbohydrates, fat, and remove action.
Daily Totals emphasizes protein over other values and never shows deficit.
Typecheck passes
Build passes
Verify in iOS simulator that the Macro tab renders all required sections without text overlap.
No unrelated files are modified
progress.txt is updated with result and notes
userStories.notes: MealLogStore @MainActor (meal_log.json): add/remove/clear, entries(on:), dailyTotals(on:). MacroView: DailyTotalsCard (InkPanel, protein emphasized, calories secondary only, NO deficit/burn), 4 action tiles (Log Meal, Add Food, New Food, Visualize Food) + Daily Totals + Food Log = all six sections. FoodLogRow shows name/time/serving amount+size/P-C-F/secondary cal/remove. NewFoodView fully implemented (manual create -> library + log). LogMeal/AddFood/Visualize are placeholders for US-009/010/012. Excluded sections omitted. 2 store tests pass; sim verified totals + log render correctly.

userStories.title: Implement Typed Meal Logging with Library-First Lookup
userStories.description: As a user, I want to type what I ate and have the app check my saved macro library before calling Gemini so that common foods are logged quickly and cheaply.
acceptanceCriteria: Typed food entry searches the local JSON macro library before any Gemini request is made.
Matching prioritizes the primary or canonical food item name and known aliases.
Known foods can be logged directly with editable serving notes and meal category.
Unknown typed foods can request a Gemini estimate and require user confirmation or editing before saving.
Confirmed Gemini estimates are saved as reusable JSON macro library records and daily log entries.
Typecheck passes
Build passes
Tests pass for known-food lookup, unknown-food fallback, confirmation-required behavior, and daily totals recalculation.
No unrelated files are modified
progress.txt is updated with result and notes

userStories.title: Implement Saved Food Search and Portion Sizing
userStories.description: As a user, I want to choose a saved food and adjust serving size so that logging repeated foods is fast and accurate enough for macro trends.
acceptanceCriteria: Saved-food search returns matching JSON library records with serving description, secondary calories, protein, carbohydrates, and fat.
The selected food view provides amount chips for 25%, 50%, 75%, exact amount, 150%, and double.
The selected food view includes a custom amount input.
Changing amount recalculates secondary calories, protein, carbohydrates, and fat before saving.
Users can cancel or log the food immediately; meal planning actions are not included in MVP.

acceptanceCriteria: Users can take or upload an acne flare photo for Gemini-supported visual review with explicit consent.

userStories.title: Build Adaptive Consult and Manual Health Assessment
userStories.description: As a user, I want adaptive consult questions and manually initiated health assessments so that I can review macros, acne, treatments, holistic practices, and patterns on demand.
acceptanceCriteria: Consult flows ask adaptive one-at-a-time questions up to 10 questions across digestion, stress, sleep, skin, cycle, food, supplements, hydration, and lifestyle.

userStories.title: Add Privacy, Consent, Export, Deletion, and MVP Verification
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear privacy and consent controls so that I understand what is stored and can manage sensitive health data.
acceptanceCriteria: Privacy settings explain local storage, optional iCloud sync, AI context use, photo analysis consent, data export, and data deletion in plain language.
Users can export personal health data where privacy constraints allow.
Users can delete personal health data and clear the macro library after explicit confirmation.
Consent is explicit for notifications, sensitive health categories, AI consult context, acne photo review, and future provider sharing if added later.
Core workflows include empty, loading, error, offline, and permission-denied states where applicable.
A verification checklist or lightweight test plan maps MVP behavior back to the PRD requirements.

Tests pass
